reasoning:
The market estimated a 78% likelihood of Khamenei remaining Supreme Leader on August 7, 2025. Our searches found that while Iran underwent a severely destabilizing "12-day war" with Israel/US around late June 2025 (causing Khamenei to shelter in a bunker and increasing rumors of his ouster or replacement), the acute phase of this conflict has ended. Khamenei made a public appearance on July 6, issued statements on July 16, and notably, made a high-level political reappointment (Ali Larijani) on August 8. The fact that he is still making appointments confirms he remains in power as of mid-August. 

With only 4.5 months remaining until the resolution date (December 31, 2025), the primary risks are natural death (he is 86 and in fragile health) or an unprecedented internal ouster (which is being discussed among Iranian elites but would be highly destabilizing and difficult to execute quickly). Given that the August 7 market price already absorbed the shock of the 12-day war and the July 30 Economist report of his "fading" power, and since he has actively governed into August, the probability should be roughly in line with the last market price, creeping up slightly due to the passage of time. I settle on 80%.
